mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly increasing the present value of equity cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of low-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, which reduces corporate profit expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of instances where the unemployment rate crosses the specified threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized strength in consumer spending and residential investment, boosting near-term corporate revenue forecasts and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series declines, or the interaction term loses statistical significance with p greater than 0.10 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects immediate improvements in manufacturing output and supply chain throughput, which triggers short-term positive market repricing and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction during tightening cycles, producing negative 5-day returns in more than 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation combined with a low federal funds rate compresses real interest rates and signals delayed monetary policy tightening, which increases equity valuation risk and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal predicts positive returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect vanishes when inflation falls below 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls directly increases aggregate household income and consumer purchasing power, supporting corporate earnings growth and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to correlate with positive forward returns with Rank IC below 0.01 across multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in real GDP below the 18500 threshold confirms economic stagnation, triggering recession risk premiums and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Markets price in the slowdown immediately, resulting in positive 60-day returns in more than 70% of sub-threshold GDP quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production and total employment signals broad-based economic expansion and rising corporate capacity utilization, which supports equity valuations and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal loses predictive power when one series diverges from the other, or forward returns are negative in more than 50% of co-expansion periods.
